title: Treasury Seeks Public Comment on GENIUS Act Notice of Proposed Rulemaking Concerning State-Level Regulatory Regimes
summary: The U.S. Department of the Treasury issued a Notice of Proposed Rulemaking (NPRM) to establish broad-based principles for determining whether state-level regulatory regimes for payment stablecoin issuers are substantially similar to the federal framework under the GENIUS Act. The NPRM is the first regulation Treasury has proposed to implement the GENIUS Act, with a 60-day public comment period.
key_points: Treasury issued NPRM establishing principles for evaluating state-level stablecoin regulatory regimes under the GENIUS Act
Stablecoin issuers with consolidated total outstanding issuance of not more than $10 billion may opt for state-level regulation if substantially similar to federal framework
This is the first regulatory proposal from Treasury implementing the GENIUS Act
Public comments invited for 60 days from Federal Register publication
Comments will be publicly viewable at regulations.gov
Builds on an advance notice of proposed rulemaking issued last September
why_it_matters: The NPRM establishes the framework for evaluating whether state-level stablecoin regulatory regimes meet the 'substantially similar' standard required under the GENIUS Act. Issuers with up to $10 billion in consolidated outstanding issuance can opt for state-level regulation if their state regime qualifies. This creates a dual-regulatory pathway that could influence which jurisdictions attract stablecoin issuance activity.
macro_context: This is regulatory policy guidance rather than a traditional macro catalyst. It represents Treasury's first concrete step to implement the GENIUS Act framework for payment stablecoin oversight. The NPRM invites public comment for 60 days following Federal Register publication.
market_structure_context: The NPRM addresses the regulatory architecture for payment stablecoins, establishing principles for determining when state-level regulatory regimes qualify as substantially similar to the federal framework. While not market plumbing in the traditional sense, this shapes the compliance landscape for stablecoin issuers and the competitive dynamics between state and federal regulatory oversight.
important_numbers: $10,000,000,000 threshold for state regulatory opt-out eligibility
